description: Add provider categorization system with category badges, filter UI, and API-key-compatible priority ordering to the providers page. Implement a ProviderCategoryBadge component showing category labels (Free, OAuth, API Key, Free Tier, Local, Search, Audio, etc.) and a filter bar enabling category-based filtering. Providers marked as API Key Compatible must be deterministically ordered at the top of the API Key section.
steps: 1. Create src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/providers/components/ProviderCategoryBadge.tsx - a small badge component that takes a providerId, calls getProviderCategory() from src/shared/constants/providers.ts, and renders a colored badge with the category label. Use shadcn Badge component with appropriate color mapping (Free=green, OAuth=blue, API Key=purple, etc.).

2. Create src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/providers/components/ProviderFilterBar.tsx - a filter bar component with category filter chips (All, Free, OAuth, API Key, Free Tier, Local, Compatible) that filters the displayed providers. Use shadcn ToggleGroup or custom chip-style buttons. Accept a value/onChange pattern for the active filter.

3. Add 'apiKeyCompatible' boolean field to relevant providers in src/shared/constants/providers.ts. Providers that accept standard OpenAI-format API keys without special auth flows should be flagged: openai, anthropic, openrouter, deepseek, groq, xai, mistral, perplexity, together, fireworks, cerebras, cohere, nebius, siliconflow, hyperbolic, opencode-go, opencode-zen, commandcode, nvidia, glm, glm-cn, kimi, minimax, minimax-cn, alicode, alicode-intl, volcengine-ark, chutes, nanobanana, blackbox. Also add 'apiKeyCompatible' to the ProviderDefinition interface.

4. Update src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/providers/page.tsx to: (a) import and render ProviderFilterBar above the provider sections, (b) add state for active filter, (c) sort API Key providers section so that apiKeyCompatible=true providers appear first (deterministic alphabetical within the compatible group), (d) apply the category filter to all sections, (e) integrate ProviderCategoryBadge into ProviderCard to show category inline.
5. Update src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/providers/components/ProviderCards.tsx to accept and render a category badge next to the provider name or below it, using ProviderCategoryBadge.

6. Add tests/unit/provider-category-filter.test.ts testing: (a) getProviderCategory returns correct category for each provider type, (b) apiKeyCompatible providers sort to top, (c) filter logic correctly filters by category.
acceptance_criteria: ProviderCategoryBadge renders correct category text and color for Free, OAuth, API Key, Free Tier, Local, Search, Audio providers
ProviderFilterBar renders filter options and filtering state changes work
API Key Compatible providers appear at the top of the API Key section deterministically
The apiKeyCompatible field is present on ~30+ providers in the APIKEY_PROVIDERS constant
New test file passes: npm run test -- provider-category-filter
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run lint passes with zero errors
